lines.description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, moderate complexity

clinicalNote: Established patient seen for management of hypertension and type 2 diabetes. Medications reviewed and adjusted; labs ordered. Moderate-complexity decision making documented.
Routine diabetes monitoring labs. Comprehensive metabolic panel drawn; creatinine billed separately on the same date of service.
scenario: Unbundling violation (component of a panel billed separately)

clinicalNote: CT of the head ordered. Documentation reflects a general adult examination with no neurological symptoms, headache, syncope, or injury recorded.
scenario: Medical-necessity mismatch (advanced imaging on a routine exam)

clinicalNote: Atrial fibrillation follow-up. Claim reports four complete electrocardiograms on a single date of service with no repeat modifier or supporting documentation.

lines.description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, low complexity

clinicalNote: Established patient, routine hypertension recheck. Stable; continue current regimen.
scenario: Original claim (paired with a later duplicate)

lines.description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, low complexity

clinicalNote: Second submission for the same member, same procedure, same date of service as claim C-1005.
Total knee arthroplasty for right-knee primary osteoarthritis. Operative side documented in the note but no laterality modifier submitted on the claim line.

lines.description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, low complexity

clinicalNote: Routine follow-up. Claim line carries modifier 'ZZ', which is not a recognized CPT/HCPCS modifier.
Total knee arthroplasty for right-knee primary osteoarthritis. Right-side laterality modifier (RT) correctly submitted.
scenario: Clean surgical claim with correct laterality modifier

clinicalNote: Baseline electrocardiogram, then a repeat ECG performed and interpreted by the same attending cardiologist after an acute symptomatic episode the same day. The repeat is billed on a separate line with modifier 76 (repeat procedure by the same physician).
scenario: Justified repeat procedure (modifier 76), must NOT be denied

lines.description: Office/outpatient visit, established patient, high complexity

clinicalNote: Established patient in for a brief, routine blood-pressure recheck. Hypertension stable on current medication, no new symptoms or complaints, no medication changes. Exam limited to vitals; straightforward, low-complexity encounter. (Synthetic claim for upcoding illustration.)
scenario: Upcoding: high-complexity E/M (99215) billed for a stable low-complexity recheck

clinicalNote: Established patient with right-knee osteoarthritis pain. The record for this date contains only the evaluation-and-management narrative: history and a focused knee exam (medial joint-line tenderness, no effusion) and the assessment/plan. CPT 20610 (arthrocentesis/injection, major joint) is billed for this date, but there is no accompanying procedure note substantiating the injection: no injection site or approach, no needle, no aspiration, and no medication name, dose, or lot recorded. The documentation on file is insufficient to support 20610 as billed; the procedure note is needed to adjudicate. (Synthetic claim: the billed procedure lacks the supporting procedure documentation; pend for records rather than an outright overpayment.)
scenario: Insufficient documentation: procedure billed without a supporting procedure note
